name: NuFACE Trinity+ Facial Toning Device with ELE & Wrinkle Reducer Attachments

specs.Technology: Microcurrent — mimics body's natural current to tone facial muscles
specs.Treatment Time: 5 min/day, 5× per week (first 60 days), then 2-3× weekly
specs.Attachments Included: ELE Attachment (eye/lip area) + Wrinkle Reducer (red light therapy)
specs.Gel: Requires NuFACE Gel Primer for conductivity

whatWorks: Microcurrent technology delivers low-level electrical current that mirrors the body's own bioelectric field. This stimulates the ATP production in facial muscles that drives toning, tightening, and re-educating sagging facial muscles. It's FDA-cleared — not just FDA-registered, which is a meaningful distinction in the at-home beauty device category where many products claim regulatory backing they don't have.

The ELE attachment's smaller applicator spheres are specifically designed for the delicate eye and lip area where the main device's sphere size is too large for precise treatment. Users who skip the ELE attachment miss out on targeted lifting around the eyes and definition around the lip border, which are often the first areas people notice laxity.

The Wrinkle Reducer attachment adds red light therapy to the microcurrent — red light at 660nm wavelength has solid peer-reviewed evidence for stimulating collagen production and reducing fine line depth. Having both modalities in one device is genuine value; standalone red light devices run $100–300 CAD separately.

The 60-day visible results timeline holds up in independent testing. Users who commit to the 5-minute daily protocol for two months consistently report visible improvements in jaw definition, brow lift, and cheek lift that hold up in before-and-after photography.
worthKnowing: The NuFACE Gel Primer is required for conductivity — without it the microcurrent cannot properly penetrate the skin. The gel costs $30–40 CAD per bottle and lasts about 30–60 days of daily use, making it an ongoing consumable that adds $180–480 CAD annually. Factor this into the total cost calculation before purchasing.

Results are maintenance-dependent, not permanent. After the initial 60-day intensive phase, you need 2–3 treatments per week to maintain the results. If you stop using the device, the toning effects reverse over several weeks. This is similar to gym results — you have to keep doing it.

At $489 CAD for the Trinity+ bundle, this is among the most expensive at-home beauty devices in this guide. Clinical in-office microcurrent treatments run $150–300 per session; the device pays for itself after 3–4 sessions if you'd otherwise seek professional treatments.

Microcurrent is contraindicated for certain conditions including pacemakers, pregnancy, epilepsy, metal facial implants, and active acne/rosacea flares. Check the full contraindication list before purchasing — NuFACE documents these thoroughly in the product manual.

commonComplaints.title: Ongoing gel cost adds up…
commonComplaints.body: The device is great but nobody warned me the gel is essentially mandatory and costs $35 a bottle. With daily use I go through about one a month. It's a significant ongoing cost.

commonComplaints.title: Results disappear if you stop using it…
commonComplaints.body: I saw real improvement at 60 days but then got busy and skipped 3 weeks. My face went back almost to where it started. You really do have to keep using it.

commonComplaints.title: The tingly sensation takes getting used to…
commonComplaints.body: The microcurrent tingling on my cheekbones was borderline uncomfortable at first. I turned the intensity down for the first two weeks and worked up. Fine now.

commonComplaints.title: Hard to see results objectively…
commonComplaints.body: I believe it works but it's hard to see improvements in your own face every day. I had to take weekly photos to convince myself it was doing something — and it was.

verdict: The NuFACE Trinity+ is the most validated at-home microcurrent device with FDA clearance and a proven 60-day results protocol. The Trinity+ bundle with ELE and Wrinkle Reducer attachments delivers more comprehensive facial toning than any comparable at-home device at or near this price. The gel consumable cost and results-maintenance dependency are real ongoing commitments — but for users who would otherwise pay for monthly professional microcurrent sessions, this device pays for itself within a few months.

realBuyers.quote: I showed my 8-week before-and-after to my dermatologist and she asked what I had done. I showed her the NuFACE. She was genuinely impressed by the jaw definition.
I've used this for 6 months. My brows are visibly higher, my jaw is tighter, and my cheeks look more lifted. I get asked if I've had work done. I haven't.
The Wrinkle Reducer attachment for red light therapy is what pushed me to the Trinity+ over the basic. The combined approach gets better results than either alone.
I was skeptical. The microcurrent tingle feels weird at first. But 3 months in I'm a convert — my face looks measurably better in photos.

sources.title: NuFACE Trinity+ Facial Toning Device — Amazon.ca

metaDescription: NuFACE Trinity+ microcurrent device review for Canada: FDA-cleared, ELE and red light therapy attachments, 60-day results protocol. ~$489 CAD on Amazon.ca.
introText: The NuFACE Trinity+ is an FDA-cleared at-home microcurrent facial toning device that includes the ELE attachment for eye and lip areas plus the Wrinkle Reducer attachment for red light therapy — a three-modality approach to facial lifting and collagen stimulation. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B0018VNJ5W) at around $489 CAD.
